title: trifolium repens subsp. orphanideum
intro: Trifolium repens subsp. orphanideum, commonly known as a subspecies of white clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Trifolium repens subsp. orphanideum include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce cholesterol levels, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Trifolium repens subsp. orphanideum are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer a range of health benefits when consumed responsibly.
